content:
I’m trying FFI, calling a C function from rust file which is present in drivers/. But I’m facing compilation time error.

ld.lld: error undefined symbol: dummy_func

referenced by helpers.c:661 (rust/helpers.c:661)
. vmlinux.o:(rust_helper_dummy_func)

ld.lld: warning <internal>:(.eh_frame) is being placed in ‘.eh_frame’
make: *** [Makefile:1337: vmlinux] Error 1

I’m trying FFI, calling a C function from rust file which is present in drivers/. But I’m facing compilation time error.

The C function needs to be non-static and the declaration provided to helpers.c. Typically you want to #include the right header at the top.

ld.lld: warning <internal>:(.eh_frame) is being placed in ‘.eh_frame’

This warning is unrelated (and being solved in Rust's side): https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/1012

Miguel Ojeda said:

I’m trying FFI, calling a C function from rust file which is present in drivers/. But I’m facing compilation time error.

The C function needs to be non-static and the declaration provided to helpers.c. Typically you want to #include the right header at the top.

ld.lld: warning <internal>:(.eh_frame) is being placed in ‘.eh_frame’

This warning is unrelated (and being solved in Rust's side): https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/1012

yes, The function is non-static and I have declared the function and header file in helpers.c . But still I'm getting the same error as above mentioned.
Also, I like to mention that my kernel function is not built-in, its module-only.
